category_title: Nonverbal elementary student with ASD who exhibits aggressive behaviors and elopement during nonpreferred activities
minimal_context: Kyle is participating in a whole-group calendar activity using a SmartBoard to review the date, days of the week, and weather.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Kyle, it is time for the group calendar activity. Please look at the SmartBoard so we can review the days of the week and the weather.

STUDENT: (Stands up from his designated area, remaining extremely active, and makes a loud vocal noise that does not serve a functional communication purpose.)

TUTOR: You need to stay seated and follow directions.

STUDENT: (Falls to the floor, continuing to make various vocal noises while refusing to look at the interactive icons on the board.)

TUTOR: Let's try to finish the calendar time.

STUDENT: (Screams and attempts to leave the classroom, protesting the activity by hitting and attempting to head-butt when approached.)

options.A: Respond to Kyle by saying, "Another one already? No way! I’m so proud of you! Good job, keep going."
options.B: Alter the original plans for the calendar activity and incorporate Kyle's contributions into the group lesson.
options.C: Turn to Kyle and say, "That’s interesting, but we are doing the calendar. Do you have something to add about the weather?"
options.D: Provide frequent prompts for Kyle to follow classroom routines, remain seated, stay in his designated area, and follow directions.
